meaning: n.群,组,多数,军队vi.群集,结队,成群而行vt.把(骑兵)编成骑兵连
examples: A troop of guests was moving towards the house.
Mr. Vlok promised new measures to protect residents, including the dispatch of police and troop reinforcements.
The troop was armed with the most advanced weapons.
When we met last year, our troop levels in Iraq were on the rise.
Secretary General Kofi Annan has scheduled a meeting for Monday afternoon with potential troop contributors.
